---
title: "LRP71: Mechanism model — letter-sound (L) -> word reading (W), moderated by expressive vocabulary (E)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP71 is the first **interaction** model in the suite. It extends LRP58 — the
mechanism estimate $f^L$ of letter-sound knowledge on word reading, fit over all
three phase transitions — with a **linear moderation** by expressive vocabulary
$E$ (`eowpvt`). The question is the lexical-quality / dual-route hypothesis:
*does the letter-sound → reading effect depend on a child's vocabulary level?*

The linear predictor adds two standardised terms alongside the existing
nonparametric $f^L$ and the adjustment terms:

$$
\eta = \dots + f^{L}(\operatorname{logit} L_{\text{post}})
       + \gamma_{\text{mod}}\, z(E)
       + \gamma_{\text{int}}\, z(\operatorname{logit} L_{\text{post}})\, z(E)
$$

where $z(\cdot)$ standardises to mean 0, sd 1 over the fitted rows.
$\gamma_{\text{int}} > 0$ means letter-sound converts to reading *more* strongly
for higher-vocabulary children. Both coefficients use the regularising
$\mathrm{Normal}(0, 0.3)$ cross-coupling prior.

_Convergence._ This model retains the nonparametric **HSGP** $f^L$ curve (matching
[LRP58](../lrp-rli-mech-058)). Two fixes brought it to a clean posterior: correcting the
adjustment-set measurement wave (#258) took it from ~90 divergences (R-hat 1.2, min ESS ≈ 9 —
genuinely unusable) to ~13, and the #265 HSGP reparameterisation (standardised GP input, a
moderate-lengthscale prior, a smaller basis) cleared the remainder — it now passes the strict
zero-divergence gate at `target_accept` = 0.999 with the curve intact.

_Reading the interaction._

- The HSGP $f^L$ is **unchanged** — it is still a flexible function of the *raw*
  $\operatorname{logit} L_{\text{post}}$. The moderation is the *linear* product
  term only; a GP-varying-slope version is deferred.
- Because $z(\operatorname{logit} L)$ is centred, $\gamma_{\text{mod}}$ is the
  vocabulary main effect *at the mean of letter-sound*, and the $f^L$ mechanism
  curve is the L → W effect *at the mean of $E$*. With the interaction present,
  $f^L$ no longer captures the full L effect across all vocabulary levels.
- With $n \approx 157$ phase-transition rows and this interaction ranking only
  mid-pack in the SHAP screen, $\gamma_{\text{int}}$ may well be *suggestive,
  not credible* — an honest, publishable result ("phonics converts to reading
  largely independently of vocabulary level, within this sample").

## Adjustment set

The revised LRP58 set (#245): $\{G, A, HS, IS(\text{attend}), SP, W_{\text{pre}}\}$.
The revision drops $EV \to LS$, so $E$ and $R$ are no longer confounders of
$L \to W$ — $E$ now enters this model **only** as the tested moderator.

- $G$ — group; always conditioned on via $\beta_G$.
- $A$ — age; conditioned on linearly by default.
- $HS$ (`hs` / `hs_missing`), $IS$ (`attend`), $SP$ (`deapp_c`) — hearing, sessions
  and speech; the revised parents of $L$. **New (#245).**
- $W_{\text{pre}}$ — baseline word reading.

_E enters as the tested moderator only._ Under the revised DAG $E$ is **no longer a confounder** of $L \to W$ (the
$EV \to LS$ edge is dropped). $E$ enters purely as the tested moderator —
$\gamma_{\text{mod}}\, z(E)$ (main effect) and $\gamma_{\text{int}}\, z(L)\, z(E)$
(interaction); the confounders of $L \to W$ are the revised set above.

_Why eowpvt and not celf._ The SHAP screen ranked `celf` (basic concepts, symbol $F$) as the strongest
candidate moderator, but LRP58's DAG treats $F$ as a **descendant of $L$**, so
conditioning on it would risk collider bias and attenuate $f^L$. `eowpvt` ($E$)
is the theory-canonical dual-route moderator **and** is already a clean DAG
adjuster, so it is the first interaction model built. The `celf` model (LRP70)
is reserved pending a DAG review.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_mechanism.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
